hero.eyebrow: Doctor of Theology (Reformed) · Clinical Psychologist · Engineer
hero.subtitle: A body of work integrating Reformed systematic theology, Lacanian psychoanalysis and logotherapy into an original interdisciplinary proposition.

author.label: The Author
author.title: Andrew Myer
author.credentials: Dr. Theol. · M.Sc. Industrial Eng. · Clinical Psychologist
author.bio_1: Andrew Myer holds a Master's degree in Industrial Engineering and a Doctorate in Reformed Theology. He maintains an independent clinical practice in which he articulates Lacanian psychoanalysis, Viktor Frankl's logotherapy, Hebrew spirituality, and Reformed theology as complementary frameworks for understanding the subject.
author.bio_2: In the theological domain, he directs a Reformed Seminary and coordinates pastoral work within the Reformed tradition. His doctoral research is grounded in classical systematic theology — with particular reference to Abraham Kuyper, Herman Bavinck, Geerhardus Vos, Cornelius Van Til, Francis Turretin, and John Frame — in dialogue with the patristic tradition, Hebrew mysticism, and the Kabbalistic tradition.
author.bio_3: He resides and practices in Chile. His work simultaneously encompasses engineering consulting, independent clinical practice, and academic direction of the Reformed Seminary, with intellectual and institutional reach toward Europe and the Mediterranean world.
author.quote: "This synthesis, developed over years of clinical practice and theoretical reflection, constitutes an original proposition in the field of psychology with a philosophical and spiritual orientation."

thought.label: Research Areas
thought.title: Interdisciplinary research
thought.subtitle: Andrew Myer's work is situated at the intersection of Reformed systematic theology, clinically oriented psychoanalytic psychology and the Western and Semitic philosophical tradition.

pillars.title: Reformed Systematic Theology
pillars.body: Research within the framework of classical Reformed theology: Abraham Kuyper, Herman Bavinck, Geerhardus Vos, Cornelius Van Til and Francis Turretin. Active lines: Christ's descent into Hades, the notion of ecclesiastical exousia, spiritual warfare from a Reformed perspective and the relationship between Hebrew mysticism and Reformed dogmatics.

pillars.title: Clinical Psychology and Theoretical Integration
pillars.body: Clinical practice integrating Lacanian psychoanalysis, Viktor Frankl's logotherapy, Hebrew spirituality and pastoral theology. Special focus on narcissism as both a clinical and philosophical-theological category, and on the mechanisms underlying the experience of human suffering.

pillars.title: Patristics, Hebrew Mysticism and Kabbalistic Theology
pillars.body: Dialogue between the Christian patristic tradition, Hebrew mysticism and Kabbalah as complementary sources of theological understanding. Research on the Kabbalistic Tzimtzum in relation to Reformed Trinitarian theology and Christology.

thought.central_quote: "Narcissism is not simply a personality disorder catalogued in diagnostic manuals, but a primordial force that has affected both celestial and earthly beings, manifesting as a universal pattern that traverses time and dimensions."

books.label: Publications
books.title: Works
books.subtitle: A bibliographic corpus integrating theological research, psychoanalytic analysis and philosophical reflection into an original interdisciplinary proposition.
books.placeholder_desc: Work in preparation in the field of systematic theology, philosophy or clinically oriented psychology.

excerpts.label: Excerpts
excerpts.title: Selected passages
excerpts.subtitle: Representative passages from the published works.

items.book: Between Two Worlds, Vol. I — Prologue
items.text: «There is a moment when the body stops obeying. It is not dramatic, at least not as one imagines. There is no sudden darkness, no curtain falling. It is more like when the sound of a full room goes silent all at once: one instant everything is there, and the next there is nothing left to hold on to.»

items.book: Between Two Worlds, Vol. I — Preface
items.text: «What my eyes saw beyond the veil —the hierarchies that govern the shadows, the extraordinary light that emanates from the throne of Adonay, the true nature of what inhabits the invisible dimensions of our reality— seemed too vast for human language.»

items.book: Between Two Worlds, Vol. I — The return
items.text: «I had learned, on the threshold between life and death, that the pain of the body and the peace of the soul can coexist. Not as a contradiction that resolves itself, but as a paradox that is inhabited.»

items.book: Narcissism: The First Law of Crystallization — Introduction
items.text: «From the Greek myth of Narcissus to the fall of the Cherub in the Hebrew tradition, these narratives provide us with a detailed map of how self-consciousness can transform into self-obsession, and how self-love can become self-destruction.»

items.book: Narcissism: The First Law of Crystallization — The universal pattern
items.text: «The fall of the Cherub was not simply an isolated event in cosmic history, but established an archetypal pattern that would resonate throughout all subsequent creation. This pattern —perfection that turns upon itself— is the First Law of Crystallisation.»

«I saw my own death multiple times, in different scenarios, as if God were showing me possible paths my life could take depending on choices I had not yet made. Not as a threat, but as a cartography of freedom.»

«That vehicle was not a body in critical condition: it was a soul descending from the celestial heights back into the flesh, traversing the liminal space that separates the eternal kingdom from the earthly plane, that invisible strip where dimensions touch and where the forces operating in both can act simultaneously.»

items.book: Jesus Christ, The Lion of Judah — The Divine Tzimtzum
items.text: «The Tallit represents Or Makif —Surrounding Light—, the protective presence of God that surrounds the soul before it descends into the physical body. In this Kabbalistic concept we find the key to understanding how the infinite God generates the ontological space for finite creation.»

items.book: The Broken Mirror — Psychoanalysis and metaphysics
items.text: «Its effectiveness lies in the fact that it operates simultaneously on the physical and spiritual plane, affecting both the body and the soul of the one who receives it, creating specific emotional and spiritual states with an efficiency that few in the physical world recognise for what it is.»

items.book: Between Two Worlds, Vol. I — The interior certitude
items.text: «The answer came not as an argument I could rebut or accept at my discretion, but as a certainty that settled in me with the same authority with which all true certainties arrive: from within, from that place where the Spirit speaks without need of words but with a clarity that exceeds any word.»
